13.title: El primer adiós
13.theme: Consolidation of A1 — preparing to leave home. Review all vocabulary and grammar. The DELE A1 silent evaluation.
13.narrative_arc: Yaguará knows all the names of the forest. But the forest is shrinking. She must decide to leave.
13.cando_statement: Can introduce themselves, ask/answer simple questions, interact simply if the other person speaks slowly.

20.title: El mercado y la calle
20.theme: Town life — shopping, transport, directions, professions. The transactional Spanish that CEFR A2 demands.
20.narrative_arc: Candelaria takes Yaguará to her town. A market, a bus station, a pharmacy. The human world has names too.
20.literary_peak: Carrasquilla — Frutos de mi tierra (town life)
20.cando_statement: Can communicate in simple routine tasks, describe their background, immediate environment, and matters of immediate need.

21.title: Prepararse para el viaje
21.theme: Planning, packing, saying goodbye. Future with ir a + infinitive. DELE A2 consolidation.
21.narrative_arc: Before crossing the threshold, they prepare. What to bring, what to leave behind. Every choice is a word.
21.literary_peak: Rivera — Arturo Cova prepara su viaje
21.cando_statement: Can communicate in simple routine tasks, describe their background, immediate environment, and matters of immediate need.

32.title: El trabajo de las manos
32.theme: Work and employment — oficina, sueldo, empresa, jefe, entrevista. How people earn their lives.
32.narrative_arc: In the towns, people work. Each profession has its own language. The fisherman speaks differently from the banker.
32.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

33.title: La red invisible
33.theme: Technology and communication — internet, teléfono, computadora, redes sociales. How the modern world connects.
33.narrative_arc: The Maestro says: technology is a tool, but the word is the soul. Candelaria discovers that stories travel faster online.
33.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

34.title: La tierra que habla
34.theme: Agriculture, food systems, rural economy. The land provides, but only if you listen.
34.narrative_arc: An agricultor shows Yaguará how food travels from field to table. Each step has a name.
34.literary_peak: Rivera — La tierra de los caucho
34.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

35.title: Cuerpos que hablan
35.theme: Health expanded — body systems, emotions, mental health. The body has its own language.
35.narrative_arc: Candelaria gets sick in the mountains. Altitude, cold, fear. The body speaks before the mouth does.
35.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

36.title: Las leyes del bosque
36.theme: Law, rights, community governance. Rules that protect and rules that destroy.
36.narrative_arc: Don Próspero has a contract. Candelaria has a constitution. Who decides what happens to the forest?
36.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

37.title: Fiestas y tradiciones
37.theme: Celebrations, traditions, cultural identity. How communities keep their names alive through ritual.
37.narrative_arc: A village fiesta. Music, dance, food, storytelling. Every tradition is a word that refuses to die.
37.literary_peak: Carrasquilla — La fiesta de San Pedro
37.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

38.title: Noticias del mundo
38.theme: Media, journalism, truth. How stories are told and distorted. Critical reading.
38.narrative_arc: A journalist visits the village. She writes one story. Don Próspero writes another. Same facts, different truths.
38.literary_peak: Rivera — El periodista en la selva
38.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

39.title: Ciencia de la selva
39.theme: Science and nature — biology, ecology, observation. The scientific names of things.
39.narrative_arc: A biologist arrives. She has Latin names for every plant. But does she know their true names?
39.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

40.title: Deportes y juegos
40.theme: Sports, games, competition, cooperation. The language of play.
40.narrative_arc: The village children play fútbol. Yaguará plays too — badly. But playing is learning.
40.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

41.title: El viaje dentro del viaje
41.theme: Travel expanded — airports, hotels, reservations, emergencies abroad. Practical survival Spanish.
41.narrative_arc: Candelaria dreams of studying abroad. She practices booking flights, reading signs, asking for help in Spanish.
41.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

42.title: Fronteras y puentes
42.theme: Borders, migration, identity. What happens when languages cross borders. DELE B1 consolidation.
42.narrative_arc: At the edge of the Mundo del Medio, borders are visible. Some are walls. Some are bridges. Language is both.
42.cando_statement: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling, produce simple connected text, describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions.

53.title: Economía de las palabras
53.theme: Economics, markets, value systems. What is the price of a name?
53.narrative_arc: Don Próspero's economics meets the forest's economy. One counts money. The other counts lives.
53.cando_statement: Can interact with a degree of fluency and spontaneity, produce clear detailed text on a wide range of subjects, explain a viewpoint on a topical issue.

54.title: Justicia y memoria
54.theme: Justice, historical memory, reconciliation. Colombia's own story of remembering.
54.narrative_arc: A truth commission arrives. They ask: what happened here? Who lost their names? Who took them?
54.literary_peak: Macías — Los que no tienen voz
54.cando_statement: Can interact with a degree of fluency and spontaneity, produce clear detailed text on a wide range of subjects, explain a viewpoint on a topical issue.

55.title: El umbral del medio
55.theme: Crossing into the upper world. DELE B2 consolidation. The bridge between earthly and cosmic language.
55.narrative_arc: The Mundo del Medio ends. Everything learned here becomes the foundation for what comes next. The bridge is words.
55.cando_statement: Can interact with a degree of fluency and spontaneity, produce clear detailed text on a wide range of subjects, explain a viewpoint on a topical issue.

66.title: El idioma como profesión
66.theme: Professional Spanish — legal, medical, business, academic register. Language as career tool.
66.narrative_arc: Candelaria is now in university. She discovers that each profession speaks its own dialect.
66.cando_statement: Can express themselves fluently and spontaneously, use language flexibly for social, academic and professional purposes, produce clear well-structured detailed text on complex subjects.

67.title: Traducir lo intraducible
67.theme: Translation, untranslatability, cultural gaps. What is lost when a word crosses languages?
67.narrative_arc: A word in Wayúu has no Spanish equivalent. What do you do? You create a bridge, not a replacement.
67.literary_peak: Macías — Lo que no se puede traducir
67.cando_statement: Can express themselves fluently and spontaneously, use language flexibly for social, academic and professional purposes, produce clear well-structured detailed text on complex subjects.

68.title: La voz del futuro
68.theme: DELE C1 consolidation. The student's voice is now their own. What will they do with it?
68.narrative_arc: Yaguará and Candelaria stand at the threshold of mastery. Below: everything they learned. Above: everything they'll create.
68.cando_statement: Can express themselves fluently and spontaneously, use language flexibly for social, academic and professional purposes, produce clear well-structured detailed text on complex subjects.

79.title: El español de América
79.theme: Dialectal variation across Latin America. Vos, tú, usted. Regionalismos. The living map of Spanish.
79.narrative_arc: Spanish is not one language. It's a constellation of voices. Each country adds a star.
79.literary_peak: Macías — El mapa de las voces
79.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

80.title: Escribir con el cuerpo
80.theme: Creative writing — poetry, short story, essay. The student becomes the author.
80.narrative_arc: The Maestro says: now write. Not to prove you know Spanish. To prove you are alive.
80.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

81.title: El humor y la ironía
81.theme: Humor, irony, sarcasm, double meaning. The highest register — making someone laugh in another language.
81.narrative_arc: The Delfín Rosado was right all along: not everything means what it seems. The joke is the deepest truth.
81.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

82.title: La música de las palabras
82.theme: Poetry analysis, prosody, rhythm, meter. Spanish as music.
82.narrative_arc: Pombo's poems have rhythm. Rivera's prose has melody. Macías's philosophy has silence. All are music.
82.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

83.title: Leer entre líneas
83.theme: Critical reading, inference, subtext, unreliable narrators. What the text doesn't say.
83.narrative_arc: Don Próspero's speeches said one thing. His actions said another. Reading between the lines is reading the truth.
83.literary_peak: Macías — Lo que no se dice
83.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

84.title: El debate y la persuasión
84.theme: Argumentation, debate, rhetoric at mastery level. Defending a position with eloquence.
84.narrative_arc: Candelaria defends the forest in a public debate. Her words are weapons of construction, not destruction.
84.literary_peak: Macías — El arte de nombrar con precisión
84.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

85.title: Mundos imaginarios
85.theme: Speculative fiction, magical realism, world-building. Creating worlds with Spanish.
85.narrative_arc: García Márquez built Macondo with words. What world will you build?
85.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

86.title: La historia que nos cuenta
86.theme: Historiography, oral history, memory. How Spanish carries the memory of continents.
86.narrative_arc: The Antiguos speak again. They tell the history of the language — from Latin to Castilian to the Americas.
86.literary_peak: Rivera — La historia de las voces
86.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

87.title: El silencio elocuente
87.theme: Silence as communication. Pauses, ellipsis, the unsaid. What happens between words.
87.narrative_arc: The Maestro's greatest lesson: sometimes the most powerful word is the one you don't say.
87.literary_peak: Macías — Piel de asno y Padremío
87.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

88.title: El árbol de las lenguas
88.theme: Multilingualism, the polyglot tree. Spanish among the world's languages. The ceiba has branches for every tongue.
88.narrative_arc: Through the ceiba's highest branches, other languages are visible. Spanish is one voice in a chorus. Learning one opens all.
88.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

89.title: Nombrar es crear
89.theme: The final destination. Full circle. The same forest. The same dawn. But you are different. Nombrar es crear.
89.narrative_arc: Dawn in the Chocó. The composition of dest1. But Yaguará radiates light. The rana holds words. Everything is named. Everything lives.
89.literary_peak: Macías — Nombrar es crear (la palabra final)
89.cando_statement: Can understand virtually everything heard or read, summarize information from different spoken and written sources, express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.
